text: Shadow of the Colossus was a launch game for the PlayStation 2.
correct_answer: False: It was released in 2005, five years after the PS2 launch.

text: Ratchet & Clank: Rift Apart was a launch title for the PlayStation 5.
correct_answer: False: It was released in June 2021, several months after the PS5 launch.

text: Metal Gear Solid originally launched on PlayStation 2.
correct_answer: False: Metal Gear Solid originally launched on the original PlayStation.

text: The PlayStation 2 supports 1080p resolution in almost all games.
correct_answer: False: Only a very small handful of games supported 1080i; most were 480i or 480p.

text: The PlayStation Vita 1000 model has a built-in 1GB of internal storage.
correct_answer: False: Only the 2000 (Slim) model had 1GB built-in; the 1000 required a memory card.

text: PaRappa the Rapper is a rhythm game first released on the PlayStation 2.
correct_answer: False: PaRappa the Rapper was a PlayStation 1 title.

text: Every PlayStation 2 model had built-in storage for saving games without a memory card.
correct_answer: False: PlayStation 2 normally used memory cards for game saves.

text: PlayStation 2 used Blu-ray discs as its normal game format.
correct_answer: False: PlayStation 2 used CDs and DVDs for games, not Blu-ray discs.

text: PlayStation 3 could not play Blu-ray movies.
correct_answer: False: PlayStation 3 could play Blu-ray movies.

text: PlayStation 3 used cartridges as its main physical game format.
correct_answer: False: PlayStation 3 used optical discs, mainly Blu-ray, not cartridges.

text: PlayStation 4 can natively play PlayStation 3 game discs.
correct_answer: False: PlayStation 4 does not natively play PS3 game discs.

text: PlayStation 4 used the Cell processor from PlayStation 3.
correct_answer: False: PlayStation 4 used an AMD x86-64 architecture, not the PS3 Cell processor.

text: PlayStation 5 uses DualShock 2 as its standard controller.
correct_answer: False: PlayStation 5 uses the DualSense controller, not DualShock 2.

text: PlayStation Home was a social 3D world on the PlayStation 2.
correct_answer: False: PlayStation Home was a social platform for the PlayStation 3.

text: PlayStation TV could play every PlayStation Vita game without exception.
correct_answer: False: PlayStation TV was compatible with only a selection of PS Vita games.

text: PlayStation Vita used UMD discs for physical games.
correct_answer: False: PS Vita used game cards, not UMD discs.

text: The PS VR2 headset requires an external camera to track movement.
correct_answer: False: PS VR2 uses inside-out tracking via cameras built into the headset.

text: PlayStation 5 Pro includes a built-in disc drive in the box.
correct_answer: False: PS5 Pro does not include a built-in disc drive; a compatible disc drive is sold separately.

text: PSP had a built-in second analog stick.
correct_answer: False: PSP had one analog nub, not two analog sticks.

text: PSP used PlayStation Vita game cards as its physical game format.
correct_answer: False: PSP used UMD discs; PS Vita used game cards.

text: Returnal is a 2D side-scrolling platformer.
correct_answer: False: Returnal is a 3D third-person roguelike shooter.

text: The PS3 'Slim' model was capable of playing Super Audio CDs (SACDs).
correct_answer: False: SACD support was removed after the early 'Fat' models.

text: The mascot character 'Crash Bandicoot' is currently owned by Sony Interactive Entertainment.
correct_answer: False: The Crash Bandicoot IP is owned by Activision (now part of Microsoft).

text: The PS5 'Create' button is located on the right side of the touchpad.
correct_answer: False: The Create button is on the left; the Options button is on the right.

text: The PlayStation 3 was the first console to feature a built-in web browser.
correct_answer: False: The Dreamcast and some PS2 configurations had browsers before the PS3.

text: The PlayStation 5 'DualSense' controller uses the same rumble technology as the DualShock 4.
correct_answer: False: The DualSense uses haptic feedback and adaptive triggers for more immersion.

text: The PlayStation 2 'Emotional Engine' refers to the console's cooling system.
correct_answer: False: The Emotion Engine was the name of the PS2's CPU.

text: The 'EyeToy' was a motion camera accessory for the PlayStation 3.
correct_answer: False: The EyeToy was for the PS2; the PS3 version was called the PlayStation Eye.

text: The original PS1 controller included vibration motors at launch.
correct_answer: False: The original controller had no vibration; this was added later with the DualShock.

text: PlayStation 5 Pro launched before the original PlayStation 5.
correct_answer: False: The original PlayStation 5 launched in 2020, while PS5 Pro launched in 2024.

text: The original PlayStation used DVD discs as its main game format.
correct_answer: False: The original PlayStation used CD-ROM discs, not DVD discs.

correct_answer: False: The original Silent Hill was a PlayStation 1 game.

text: The PlayStation 3 was the first console in the series to use a controller with a built-in touchpad.
correct_answer: False: The PlayStation 4 DualShock 4 was the first to feature a touchpad.

text: The 'PocketStation' was a memory card peripheral with a LCD screen for the PlayStation 2.
correct_answer: False: The PocketStation was a peripheral specifically for the PlayStation 1.

text: Infamous: Second Son features a protagonist named Cole MacGrath.
correct_answer: False: The protagonist of Second Son is Delsin Rowe.

text: The PlayStation 1 used standard 3.5-inch floppy disks for game saves.
correct_answer: False: The PS1 used proprietary 1MB Memory Cards.

text: The PlayStation 4 was released in Japan before it was released in North America.
correct_answer: False: The PS4 launched in NA in Nov 2013, and Japan in Feb 2014.

text: The PlayStation 4 uses a PowerPC-based processor.
correct_answer: False: The PS4 uses an x86-64 AMD Jaguar processor.

text: The 'Sixaxis' controller for PS3 featured rumble vibration at its launch.
correct_answer: False: The Sixaxis lacked vibration; it was reintroduced later with the DualShock 3.

text: The PlayStation Vita used standard Micro SD cards for memory expansion.
correct_answer: False: The Vita used expensive, proprietary PlayStation Vita Memory Cards.

text: Tomb Raider was originally released exclusively on PlayStation.
correct_answer: False: Tomb Raider was not originally exclusive to PlayStation; it also appeared on other platforms.

text: Every PlayStation 4 game is playable on PlayStation 5 through backward compatibility.
correct_answer: False: While 99% are compatible, a very small number of titles are not supported.

text: Tekken 1 was a launch title for the PlayStation 1 in North America.
correct_answer: False: While it came early, it was not a launch day title in North America.

text: The PlayStation 5 allows you to play games directly from an external USB Hard Drive.
correct_answer: False: You can only play PS4 games from USB; PS5 games must be on the internal SSD or M.2 SSD.
